description: U.S. Army facility in Texas
description_wiki: Camp Fannin was a U.S. Army Infantry Replacement Training Center and prisoner-of-war camp located in between Tyler, Texas, and Kilgore, Texas.

description: city in and county seat of Smith County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Tyler is a city in and the county seat of Smith County, Texas, United States.

description_wiki: Oregon (  ORR-ih-ghən, -⁠gon) is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: Austronesian language; native language of the Tagalog ethnic group
description_wiki: Tagalog ( tə-GAH-log, native pronunciation: [tɐˈɡaːloɡ] ; Baybayin: ᜆᜄᜎᜓᜄ᜔) is an Austronesian language spoken as a first language by the ethnic Tagalog people, who make up a quarter of the population of the Philippines, and as a second language by the majority.

aliases: Dia da Vitória sobre o Japão
Dia da Vitória sobre o Japão (1945)
description: day on which Japan surrendered, effectively ending World War II
description_wiki: Victory over Japan Day (also known as V-J Day, Victory in the Pacific Day, or V-P Day) is the day on which Imperial Japan surrendered in World War II, in effect bringing the war to an end.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: island in the Philippines
description_wiki: Leyte ( LAY-tee, LAY-tay, Tagalog: [ˈleite]) is an island in the Visayas group of islands in the Philippines.

description: largest island of the Philippines
description_wiki: Luzon ( loo-ZON, Tagalog: [luˈson]) is the largest and most populous island in the Philippines.

aliases: Queen City of the Pacific and others
description: capital city of the Philippines
description_wiki: Manila, officially the City of Manila, is the capital and second-most populous city of the Philippines after Quezon City.

seat of Clark County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Vancouver (  van-KOO-vər) is a city in Clark County, Washington, United States, located on the north bank of the Columbia River.

description: PAF Angeles
description_wiki: Clark Air Base is a Philippine Air Force base in Luzon, located 3 miles (4.8 km) west of Angeles City, and about 40 miles (64 km) northwest of Metro Manila.

aliases: United States Air Force Thirteenth Air Force
description: former United States Air Force numbered air force
numbered air force of the United States Air Force
description_wiki: The Fifth Air Force (5 AF) is a numbered Air Force of the United States Air Force Pacific Air Forces (PACAF).

description: largest island of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States of America
Pacific island, part of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States
archipelago in western North Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Mariana Islands ( MARR-ee-AH-nə; Chamorro: Manislan Mariånas), also simply the Marianas, are a crescent-shaped archipelago comprising the summits of fifteen longitudinally oriented, mostly dormant volcanic mountains in the north-western Pacific Ocean, between the 12th and 21st parallels north and along the 145th meridian east.

description: city of the Philippines
description_wiki: Baguio (UK:  BAG-ee-oh, US:  BAH-ghee-oh, -⁠OH, Tagalog: [ˈbaɡjo]), officially the City of Baguio (Ibaloi: Siudad ne Bagiw; Ilocano: Siudad ti Baguio; Tagalog: Lungsod ng Baguio), is a  highly urbanized city in the Cordillera Administrative Region, Philippines.

description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description_wiki: Northwestern University (NU) is a private research university in Evanston, Illinois, United States.
